title: MacroSnap — Free Access for Coach's Own Client Account (v2 Finalized)

children.title: Validate complementary MS license is granted automatically on new client creation (email match + paid WS)
Validate complementary license is NOT counted in subscription seat total and NOT visible on MS management page
Validate revocation triggers: coach removed from WS, WS downgraded to Starter (archive is NOT a trigger)
Validate dual-license reconciliation: client assigned via MS management first, then added as teammate — license counted; unassign → complementary takes over
Validate UI disabled button + tooltip on client profile when WS has active MS subscription; button hidden when MS cancelled
Validate migration job grants retroactive complementary MS to all eligible existing accounts; email NOT sent for WL WS
Validate pre-release manual assign is reconciled and manual unassign remains unassigned post-migration
QA-internal: Verify archived client does NOT accidentally trigger grant/revoke; confirm migration email query handles archived clients correctly
In Scope
Complementary license grant: invite path, add teammate path (both dual-license and non-dual-license)
Retroactive migration grant job (existing eligible accounts)
Revocation: coach removal from WS, WS downgrade to Starter
Dual-license reconciliation (BR-13): MS management assign → add teammate → manual unassign
Seat count integrity (complementary license not counted)
MS management page and assignment dropdown visibility
State transitions: client (connected/archived/pending/deleted), coach (pending/active/deleted), WS plan (paid/Starter), MS subscription (active/cancelled)
Email notification: retroactive grant email (one-time, non-WL only); no email for new post-deployment or WL WS accounts
UI: disabled button + tooltip on client profile (MS subscription active)
UI: button hidden on client profile (MS subscription cancelled)
UI callout for paid plan coaches (perk awareness)
UI callout for Free/Trial plan coaches (upsell)
Migration data matrix: coach plan x client role x client status x pre-release license state permutations
White-label WS: grant eligibility (Yes); email suppression (Yes)
QA-internal: archived client does NOT trigger revoke/re-grant
Out of Scope
Archive/unarchive as revocation/re-grant triggers (removed from product scope in v2)
Regular MS license purchase and assignment flows (not changed)
MS feature functionality within client app (nutrition tracking etc.)
Claim package activation path (pending BE/PM confirmation)
Enterprise / non-Self-Serve WS plan behavior
Integration Testing
Grant engine: eligibility check across all activation paths
Revocation handler: coach removal, WS downgrade
Dual-license reconciliation: MS management assign → add teammate → unassign
State transition events: client and coach lifecycle hooks
Migration job: retroactive grant + email with WL suppression
System Testing
End-to-end grant → use → revoke flow
End-to-end dual-license → unassign → complementary access retained
Seat count before/after migration; before/after dual-license unassign
MS management page visibility across all scenarios
Email delivery, WL suppression, and deduplication
UI/UX Testing
Client profile: disabled button state with tooltip (MS subscription active)
Client profile: button hidden state (MS subscription cancelled)
Perk callout on MS page for paid WS coaches
Upsell callout for Free/Trial coaches
Upgrade Everfit plan link navigation
Regression Testing
Existing regular MS assignment/unassignment flows (non-complementary clients)
MS management page counts for non-complementary licenses
WS plan change flows (upgrade/downgrade)
QA-internal: archived client state — no accidental revoke/re-grant
Sanity Test
Create connected client → verify MS access in client app
Remove coach from WS → verify MS access revoked
Verify MS management page seat count unchanged after grant
Verify disabled button + tooltip on client profile
Functional Testing
Verify all 7 canonical flows (F1–F7) from Core Business Flow
Verify all 16 canonical business rules (BR-01 to BR-16)
Verify all 5 user concerns (Concerns #1–#5)
Verify 4 meeting-decision changes (archive removal, disabled UI, dual-license, WL email)
Testing Types
State Transition Testing — client, coach, WS plan, MS subscription lifecycle
Migration Testing — pre-release data reconciliation + dual-license cleanup
Negative Testing — ineligible plans, role-change non-trigger, WL email suppression
Boundary Testing — plan change timing, email case sensitivity, MS cancellation timing
Race Condition Testing — concurrent migration + plan change; dual-license simultaneous grant
Test Design Techniques
Decision Table — WS plan x coach role x client state x email match x WL flag
State Transition Matrix — all valid/invalid transitions for client, coach, WS plan, MS subscription
Equivalence Partitioning — paid/non-paid; Owner/Admin/Trainer; standard/WL; manual assigned/unassigned/none
Error Guessing — pre-release states, dual-license, concurrent operations, archived client
Pairwise Testing — migration data matrix permutations
Automation Approaches
P0 grant/revoke flows — automate as regression candidates
Seat count integrity check — automate with DB assertion
State transitions — automate state machine coverage
UI disabled button state — automate client profile checks
WS Plan Variants
Pro plan WS (standard)
Studio plan WS
Accelerator Bundle WS
Starter plan WS (ineligible)
Free Trial WS (ineligible)
White-label WS on paid plan (grant eligible, email suppressed)
Coach Role Variants
Owner (eligible)
Admin (eligible)
Trainer (eligible, same as Owner/Admin)
Admin → Trainer role change (no revocation)
Coach in pending state → transition to active
Coach removed from WS (revocation trigger)
Client State Variants
Connected client (email matches active Owner/Admin/Trainer)
Archived client with matching email (QA-internal: no grant/revoke)
Pending client with matching email → transition to connected
Client with non-matching email
Client who is also Owner of WS (same email)
Client who is also Trainer in WS
Client who is also Admin in WS
MS Subscription States
WS with active MS subscription (button disabled state)
WS with cancelled MS subscription (button hidden state)
WS without MS subscription (no button; perk callout)
Pre-release Migration Data
Coach WITHOUT MS plan; connected client with matching email
Coach WITH MS plan; connected client assigned regular MS (pre-release)
Coach WITH MS plan; connected client had regular MS manually un-assigned (pre-release, must remain unassigned)
Dual-license: client assigned via MS management, then added as teammate (same email)
Archived client (Owner/Trainer/Admin) with matching email
Pending client (Owner/Trainer/Admin) with matching email
Connected client (Owner/Trainer/Admin) — all 3 role variants
Email Data
Standard WS accounts before deployment (retroactive email expected)
WL WS accounts before deployment (retroactive grant expected; no email)
Accounts created after deployment (no email expected)
Mixed-case email variants for case-sensitivity boundary testing
Environments
Staging — primary test environment
Production data scan — pre-migration read-only analysis
White-label WS instance — WL grant + email suppression verification
Tools
Jira — PAY-2556, PAY-2557 traceability
Postman / API client — direct grant/revoke API verification
DB query tools — seat count and license state verification
Email testing tool (Mailtrap or equivalent) — email notification + WL suppression
Playwright — automation for P0 regression flows
Functional Exit Criteria
All P0 scenarios pass: grant, revoke, seat count, management page, disabled UI button, WL email suppression, manual-unassign preservation
Dual-license reconciliation (BR-13) verified: regular license counted → unassign → complementary access retained
All 5 user concerns have confirmed expected behavior and test results, including Trainer eligibility and manual-unassign preservation
Migration job tested against all data permutations with correct outcomes
QA-internal archive state verification completed: no unintended revoke/re-grant on archive
No P0 or P1 open bugs at release; P2 bugs documented and accepted
Non-functional Exit Criteria
Migration job completes without data corruption on staging
Seat count consistent before/after migration
No race condition failures on concurrent plan change + migration
Email deduplication verified: no double-send on migration retry
Documentation Exit Criteria
HANDOFF.md updated with session status and open items
TEST_MATRIX.md updated with coverage per module
All blocking clarifications resolved and documented
Migration test matrix documented and signed off
high-level-strategy-analysis.md — v2 finalized strategy upstream input
ms-free-access-test-strategy.xmind — v2 visual strategy map
TEST_MATRIX.md — module coverage tracking
HANDOFF.md — session continuity document
Migration test matrix — data permutation expected outcomes
Detailed test cases (via qa-master-workflow pipeline)
